generated_oml:
// C3: Physical sensing components
instance TemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Temperature sensor defined in the enriched BIM model, publishing real‑time measurements via Eclipse Hono to Eclipse Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:producedData TemperatureDataTransmission
]

instance CO2Sensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "CO₂ sensor defined in the enriched BIM model, publishing real‑time concentration values via Eclipse Hono to Eclipse Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CO2DataTransmission
]

instance LightLevelSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Ambient light sensor defined in the enriched BIM model, publishing real‑time illuminance values via Eclipse Hono to Eclipse Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightLevelDataTransmission
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction (DataTransmitted)
instance TemperatureDataTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom TemperatureSensor
]

instance CO2DataTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CO2Sensor
]

instance LightLevelDataTransmission : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightLevelSensor
]

// C10: Data (from sensors)
instance TemperatureData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Stream of temperature values (°C) arriving at least once per second, validated by Ditto policies for units and ranges."
    DTDFVocab:fromData TemperatureDataTransmission
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HonoIngestion
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoMiddleware
]

instance CO2Data : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Stream of CO₂ concentration values (ppm) arriving at least once per second, validated by Ditto policies."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CO2DataTransmission
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HonoIngestion
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoMiddleware
]

instance LightLevelData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Stream of illuminance values (lux) arriving at least once per second, validated by Ditto policies."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LightLevelDataTransmission
    DTDFVocab:inputTo HonoIngestion
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DittoMiddleware
]

// C11: Enablers
instance HonoIngestion : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono ingestion layer that receives at‑least‑once MQTT/AMQP messages from physical sensors."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeSyncService
]

instance DittoMiddleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Ditto platform that stores, validates policies and provides bidirectional synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:enables MonitoringService
    DTDFVocab:enables ActuationControlService
]

instance MDEExtraction : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model‑Driven Engineering toolchain that extracts semantic representations from the enriched BIM model and generates deployable DT artifacts."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeploymentAutomationService
]

instance UnrealEngineVisualization : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unreal Engine 5 visualization plugin that renders the digital twin and receives updates from Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualizationService
]

instance BaSyxDeployment : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Open‑source BaSyx platform used to host and expose the generated digital twin artifacts."
    DTDFVocab:enables DeploymentAutomationService
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeSyncService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real‑time data exchange service ensuring synchronization of sensor updates at least once per second."
    DTDFVocab:provides FacilityStateInsight
]

instance MonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Monitoring service that tracks device states and stores events in Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyInsight
]

instance ActuationControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service that routes control commands from UE5 to physical actuators via Ditto."
    DTDFVocab:provides AdjustLightingAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Interactive 3‑D visualization service for facility managers."
    DTDFVocab:provides FacilityStateInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides IssueAlertAction
]

instance CustomAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Extension point allowing DT engineers to plug in analytics, optimization and prediction services."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyEfficiencyInsight
]

instance DeploymentAutomationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated deployment pipeline that assembles DT artifacts from the enriched BIM model."
    DTDFVocab:provides FacilityStateInsight
]

// C17: Insights / Actions
instance FacilityStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Provides up‑to‑date operational metrics visualized in UE5 for on‑the‑fly decision making."
]

instance EnergyEfficiencyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Analyzes sensor data to suggest energy‑saving actions."
]

instance AdjustLightingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Command to set lighting actuator on/off, brightness level and color temperature."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance IssueAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generates an alert when sensor values exceed policy‑defined thresholds."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C2: Acting Components
instance LightingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "BIM‑modeled lighting actuator exposing on/off, brightness level and color temperature attributes, controlled via Ditto."
]

// C10: Models/Data (static BIM data)
instance EnrichedBIMGeometryModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Geometric foundation of the twin derived from the enriched BIM model, containing building geometry and device placement."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MDEExtraction
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BaSyxDeployment
]

instance DeviceBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Behavioral model of sensors, actuators and control logic authored by building control engineers."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MDEExtraction
    DTDFVocab:inputTo BaSyxDeployment
]

instance BIMMetadata : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Static metadata extracted from BIM describing devices, locations and attributes."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MDEExtraction
]

instance ControlLogicData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Control rules and setpoints defined by building control engineers."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MDEExtraction
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The digital twin can send control commands back to the building’s control systems via the framework’s bidirectional exchange. Facility Managers interact with the DT to initiate actions, and the integrated services route the issued commands to the physical devices. Specific command formats, validation, and authentication mechanisms are handled by the services but are not detailed in the provided documents."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The framework implements real‑time data exchange that continuously synchronises the physical building with its digital twin; the documents state the exchange is real‑time but do not provide concrete synchronization intervals, latency limits, or temporal granularity."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The system follows a modular, model‑driven architecture where enriched BIM models are transformed via MDE techniques into deployable DT artifacts; a central DT Framework provides standardized extension points, runtime configuration management, and bidirectional data exchange, enabling integration of custom services and analytics while managing resources at the component level."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The framework defines a full DT life‑cycle that starts with device modeling inside the BIM environment, where sensors, actuators and controllers are defined (functional requirement F1). Enriched BIM models are then transformed by model‑driven engineering (MDE) into structured DT artifacts. An automated deployment process (R2) generates reproducible runtime instances, establishing the transition from design‑time models to deployed twins. At runtime the twin maintains real‑time data exchange with the physical asset and provides interactive visualization for monitoring and control. The modular architecture with defined extension points (R4) enables later integration of custom services, allowing the twin to evolve while preserving the original semantic foundation. Governance is addressed by the automated, reproducible deployment and the modular extension mechanism."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Model fidelity is ensured through an automated pipeline that includes a Model‑Checking stage (5.53 s) and Model‑Parsing stage (8.7 s) before initialization. Eclipse Ditto enforces policy‑based validation of sensor values (range and unit checks) and pre‑execution validation of actuator commands to prevent conflicts. Fault tolerance is provided by Eclipse Hono’s at‑least‑once delivery and Ditto’s persistent event storage, mitigating network or service disruptions. The overall synchronization quality is quantified using the Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE) metric, with measured scores of Timeliness = 0.997, Reliability = 1.0, Availability = 1.0, indicating high fidelity and reliable data exchange. These mechanisms collectively constitute the quality assurance process for models and data in the Digital Twin."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Interoperability is addressed through non‑functional requirements for heterogeneous system integration and by exposing standardized extension points that allow external services to connect to the DT Framework; integration relies on BIM data and generic data‑exchange mechanisms, but no specific protocols (e.g., MQTT, OPC‑UA) or enterprise standards are detailed."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The approach is built around BIM, the de‑facto standard modelling paradigm in AECO, and leverages open‑source frameworks such as Eclipse Ditto and Eclipse Hono that implement widely adopted IoT connectivity standards (e.g., MQTT, JSON). The modular architecture defines standardised extension points to assure interoperability, and the automated deployment pipeline enforces a repeatable, standards‑compliant artefact generation process. No explicit certification or external industry standard references are given beyond BIM and the mentioned open‑source IoT standards."
]
